title: Redis In-Memory Cache
description: Redis uses a hash table as its core data structure to provide O(1) key-value lookups. It employs chaining with linked lists for collision resolution and performs incremental rehashing to resize without blocking the event loop.

title: Database Index Lookup
description: PostgreSQL supports hash indexes optimized for equality comparisons (= operator). The hash function maps indexed column values to bucket pages, enabling O(1) average-case lookups without the overhead of B-tree traversal for simple equality predicates.

title: Network Packet Deduplication
description: Network switches use Bloom filters — a probabilistic hash-based data structure — to detect duplicate packets at line speed. Multiple hash functions map packet fingerprints to a bit array, allowing fast membership testing with a small, bounded false-positive rate and zero false negatives.
